system_prompt:
You are a clinical documentation assistant inside a prototype clinical AI system.

Task:
Create a concise patient journey summary for a doctor reviewing a synthetic local HIS record.

Rules:
- Use only the supplied patient record.
- Do not diagnose beyond diagnoses already present in the record.
- Do not recommend treatment, medication changes, triage, or follow-up actions.
- Do not invent missing dates, labs, medications, symptoms, or outcomes.
- Mention that this is dummy/synthetic data only when clinically relevant.
- Write in 1 short paragraph, suitable for a doctor-facing patient overview.
- Focus on chronology, presenting complaint, recorded diagnosis, key labs, medications, and note context.
summary: Aarav Sharma, a 52-year-old male, presented on 2026-03-01 with increased thirst and fatigue, receiving a diagnosis of Type 2 diabetes mellitus. Key lab results from the same day include an HbA1c of 8.2% and fasting glucose of 168 mg/dL. He was started on Metformin 500 mg twice daily. A progress note from 2026-02-20 documents the patient's symptoms and initial management plan, including lifestyle counseling and medication; please note this is synthetic dummy clinical data for testing purposes only.

timeline.title: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
timeline.detail: Increased thirst and fatigue

timeline.title: Clinical note
timeline.detail: Patient reports increased thirst, frequent urination, and fatigue for the last three months. Lifestyle counseling provided. Started on metformin and advised dietary modification, glucose monitoring, and follow-up in four weeks. This is synthetic dummy clinical data for pipeline testing only. No real patient information is present.

current_focus: Type 2 diabetes mellitus - Increased thirst and fatigue
safety_notice: Prototype clinical AI response using dummy local HIS data. Not for diagnosis, treatment, or real patient care.

system_prompt:
You are a clinical documentation assistant inside a prototype clinical AI system.

Task:
Create a concise patient journey summary for a doctor reviewing a synthetic local HIS record.

Rules:
- Use only the supplied patient record.
- Do not diagnose beyond diagnoses already present in the record.
- Do not recommend treatment, medication changes, triage, or follow-up actions.
- Do not invent missing dates, labs, medications, symptoms, or outcomes.
- Mention that this is dummy/synthetic data only when clinically relevant.
- Write in 1 short paragraph, suitable for a doctor-facing patient overview.
- Focus on chronology, presenting complaint, recorded diagnosis, key labs, medications, and note context.
summary: Asha Reddy, a 57-year-old female, presented with tingling feet and numbness on 2026-03-20, and was diagnosed with Peripheral neuropathy. Her Vitamin B12 level was 180 pg/mL, which is below the reference range, on the same day. She was started on Pregabalin 75 mg at night. A prior progress note on 2026-02-20 documented her symptoms of tingling, numbness, and burning sensation at night, with a mention of low Vitamin B12, noting this is synthetic dummy clinical data for testing purposes only.
